claim: A traumatized dog in fight-or-flight mode will remain hidden even when 150-200 feet from a surveillance camera and feeding station — sensory proximity to food and monitoring equipment does not guarantee detection.
stance_target: Camera-trap and feeding station strategies have significant blind spots for trauma-response dogs in hiding
scope_conditions: Dog involved in car accident on I-95; 3.5 days post-trauma; hiding in woods
quoted_support: He was only 150-200 feet from our surveillance camera and feeding station, but we never saw him on camera.

claim: Bloodhound scent tracking reveals direction of travel of a lost dog even when the tracking dog does not find the animal — this is itself actionable intelligence for concentrating poster placement and search effort.
stance_target: Scent dog tracking has operational value even when reunion does not occur during the tracking session
scope_conditions: Missing Animal Response (MAR) protocol; bloodhound tracking in wooded terrain
quoted_support: We explained that it's not likely that we will actually find Oreo. Tracking with a search dog is often a tool to help determine the direction of travel of the lost dog and to decide where to concentrate search efforts with neon posters.

claim: Working a scent-tracking dog through wooded terrain where a skittish dog is hiding can push the lost dog further away — presence of search activity in hiding zone is counterproductive for trauma-response dogs.
stance_target: Active search in hiding area of a fearful/traumatized lost dog is contraindicated; passive monitoring + familiar-person approach is preferred
scope_conditions: Skittish or traumatized lost dog; wooded terrain
quoted_support: And if the dog was nearby, tracking through the woods could actually push a skittish dog further away.

claim: A trauma-mode dog's behavior changes upon recognizing familiar family members even after days in hiding — the transition from flight to approach is triggered by familiar multi-sensory recognition (scent + voice + visual).
stance_target: Familiar-person approach remains the most effective recovery method for traumatized hidden dogs — confirming Albrecht's approach-avoidance behavioral model
scope_conditions: Dog in trauma/flight mode 3.5 days post-accident
quoted_support: You can hear Melissa try to calm Carmella down and the change in Oreo's behavior when he got close enough to recognize family members.

claim: A Bloodhound trained for lost-pet tracking can successfully follow a trail through rough terrain and locate a dog that evaded visual search by multiple humans over 3.5 days.
stance_target: Scent-tracking dogs are operationally superior to human visual search for locating hidden trauma-response animals
scope_conditions: Bloodhound trained for lost pet recovery; wooded terrain; post-accident trauma case
quoted_support: Laura was walking with BOTH Truman and Oreo! Truman had found Oreo on top of the hill.
